card.intuition: The practice of checking user input against a set of rules (Validation) and cleaning it (Sanitization) to ensure it is safe before processing it.
card.analogy: A bouncer at a club: Validation is checking if the person has an ID and is over 21. Sanitization is making them leave their weapons (malicious scripts) at the door before they enter.
when_to_use: Every single time your application receives data from the outside world (API calls, forms, URL params)
Standardizing data formats before saving them to the database
Preventing XSS and SQL injection at the entry point
gotchas: Never trust client-side validation alone! Attackers can easily bypass your JavaScript and send malicious data directly to your API
Don't just blacklist bad characters; it's much safer to 'whitelist' allowed characters

exercise.prompt: Identify the difference between Validation and Sanitization.
exercise.test_code:
assert "Val" in tasks["Check if the email contains an @ symbol"]
assert "San" in tasks["Strip <script> tags from a comment string"]
print("PASS")
